name: 250710-05 Interior Design.pdf
description:
**VCC: NOT INTERIOR WORK ONLY. VCC REVIEW AND APPROVAL ARE REQUIRED.

THE PROJECT SCOPE IS RESTRICTED TO INTERIOR RENOVATIONS OF SELECT SPACES THROUGHOUT THE SUB-BASEMENT, BASEMENT, LEVEL 01, LEVEL 02 & LEVEL 03 AT THE ROYAL SONESTA LOCATED AT 300 BOURBON ST. IN NEW ORLEANS, LA., AND DOES NOT AFFECT THE EXISTING OCCUPANCY, EXISTING OCCUPANCY COUNTS OR EXISTING EGRESS ROUTES. SPACES INCLUDED IN THE RENOVATION ARE OUTLINED BELOW:

SUB-BASEMENT: ROYAL/CONTI, BACCHUS, BOURBON, CORRIDOR, & STAIR 12, BASEMENT STAIR 12, FITNESS ROOM, WOMENS & MENS RR, MOTOR LOBBY

LEVEL 1: MAIN BALLROOM, PREFUNCTION, EVANGELINE (A, B, C), VESTIBULE, REGAL ROOM, ARCADE, ESPLANADE, IBERVILLE, SOLEIL & SOULE, ENTRANCE LOBBY, HOTEL LOBBY, LE BOOZE, JAZZ PLAYHOUSE, COURTYARD, FLEUR DE LIS SALON, FLEUR DE LIS (A, B, C), DESIRE OYSTER BAR, WOMENS & MENS RR.

LEVEL 2: BOARD ROOM, TECHE, BELLE GROVE, OAK ALLEY, MADEWOOD, GRIS GRIS, CHOCTAW, ASSEMBLY AREA, & TERRACE
 
LEVEL 3: POOL BAR
             
REFERENCE SHEET PS-G004.00 FOR A DIAGRAM OF AREA OF RENOVATION SCOPE THAT IS A LEVEL 1 ALTERATION & LEVEL 2 ALTERATION.

THE HOTEL IS TO REMAIN FULLY OPERATIONAL WITH ALL LIFE SAFETY SYSTEMS FULLY OPERATIONAL DURING CONSTRUCTION. 

ALL RESTROOM FIXTURE COUNTS ARE EXISTING TO REMAIN AND WILL NOT BE ALTERED AS PART OF THE INTERIOR RENOVATION.

THE ORIGINAL STRUCTURE DATES TO THE MID-1860'S, AND INCLUDED RESIDENTIAL SLEEPING QUARTERS AND A CHAPEL.   IT'S CURRENT USE INCLUDES A DEFUNCT KITCHEN, MEETING ROOMS AND OFFICES.   THE SCOPE OF WORK FOR THE PROJECT IS TO REDEVELOP THE FIRST FLOOR WITH A NEW KITCHEN (INCLUDING AN EXPANSION OF THE KITCHEN), FIRST FLOOR OFFICES, SECOND FLOOR SLEEPING ROOMS (WITH A MAXIMUM OF 4 BEDS PER ROOM.

name: 21_0618 St Joseph Hotel Electrical Permit Set.pdf
description:
Proposal for new hotel within the footprint of an existing vacant storage building first used as a brewery. Site and building have long since been abandoned and new hotel will allow for adaptive re-use of the historic brewery structure. New construction will require full Demolition of 1 story warehouse building facing N. Diamond St and partial demotion of the existing 2 story warehouse building facing St Joseph St with retention of the front façade including cornice, windows, and exterior doors. 

The project consists of preserving the 2 story, 37’ tall, masonry building envelope along St. Joseph Street and building a new 5 story structure behind the historic building. This 5 story new structure is proposed from property line to property line on the Tchoupitoulas, S. Peters, and N. Diamond sides and proposes a 10’ setback from the St. Joseph Street façade on the 4th story and an additional 10’ setback at the 5th floor for a total setback of 20’. The new structure shall include a 5 level, 96 key hotel, a public lobby bar/restaurant, a rooftop bar/restaurant, and a rooftop pool that is open to the public.

name: 2022-10-24 St Andrew Civil Plans.pdf
description: New Construction Planned Unit Development (P.U.D.) on a subdivided lot (originally 1721-35 St. Andrew St. and to be changed to 1725 St. Andrew St. Units A-P). Building 1 consists of seven (7) attached townhouse units (B-H) and is part of a Planned Unit Development project with 16 total units. Building 1 is one of four separate buildings on this P.U.D. project site. Attached Townhomes are 2 stories, with ground floor garage and driveway, and contain 2 bedrooms & 2 bathrooms each. Construction system consists of heavy timber structural frame, concrete grade beam foundation, CMU wall base, Structurally Insulated Panels (SIP), & Steel Stud walls, floors, and roof. Painted fiber cement board exterior wall finish and standing seam metal roof. Attached townhomes are sprinklered and each unit is separated by 1-hour rated demising walls.

name: Norman C Francis Site Plan2_5.11.23 .pdf
description: As part of Prospect New Orleans the Artists of Public Memory is a new art commission and community engagement art project. Kai Lumumba Barrow will construct an “abolitionist playground,” bringing attention to sites of carceral control while engaging counter-narratives of play and creative imagination. The project will consist of a series of sculptural installations that give physical form to the institutional ramifications of racism and modes of survival and resistance. The sculptures will take the form of a boardwalk that intersects the existing pedestals for former monuments on Norman C Francis Parkway, sections of doors that interrupt the pathway, desired paths the stem from one pedestal and meander towards former Jeff Davis monument pedestal which will appear as ruins or rubble. The project is intended to remain up for 6 months at which point the neutral ground will be restored.

name: 24022 Jefferson Church Ph1 Mech Permit Set Rev1 07-08-24. (RCC stamped).pdf
description: Phase One encompasses work required to make the facilities ready for a new congregation. This includes an overhaul of the mechanical systems serving the Educational Building and Gym/Fellowship Hall including both modified and entirely new systems. In addition to accounting for the power needs of new mechanical systems, the electrical scope includes swapping out existing light fixtures with new fixtures in the same location and providing new lighting and outlets to support services hosted in the Fellowship Hall. Plumbing upgrades include the relocation of an existing laundry room and the addition of two small restrooms in an area prior utilized as storage. Phase One also aims to address the roofing scope campus-wide to ensure watertight buildings before future improvements take place. Beyond system and envelope upgrades, interior finishes in these areas of the building will also be upgraded to avoid any future work requirements to the spaces in later phases of construction.

name: Ideal Gas Station Architectural MEP Set_9-20-2024.pdf
description: The scope of work consists of structurally renovating an existing convenience store building, renovating the pump island fascias, and installing new signage. The existing building will be about 50% partially demolished by removing the existing roof framing and some interior non-load-bearing walls. An addition to increase the Convenience Store sales area will be constructed on the left side of the building and a new corrugated metal roof will be constructed. The existing CMU walls will remain in place. A knee wall will be built to raise the ceiling height from 9 feet to 14 feet. The old shed building on the left side will be removed to allow for the addition to the existing Convenience Store. New landscaping and sidewalk repair is proposed. Existing fuel pumps will remain. No new pumps will be installed. The owner is only painting the columns and repairing the canopy ceilings and replacing the fascia with new metal panels and new signage.

name: 801_Norman_C_Francis_-Framing(2nd floor)-(Report)-Building-02_01_2025-24-30222-rnvs_-N_C.pdf
description:
THE INTENT OF THIS PROJECT IS TO PERFORM INTERIOR AND EXTERIOR REPAIRS RESULTING FROM A COMBINATION OF FOUNDATION AND ENVELOPE ISSUES. THE SCOPE OF WORK INCLUDES:
PILE REPAIR 
o	REPAIR OF COMPRIMISED PILES CONCENTRATED IN THE NORTHEAST CORNER OF THE BUILDING
STAIR TOWER REPAIR
 o	STRUCTURAL AND ENVELOPE REPAIRS TO THE NORTHEAST STAIR TOWER ADDITION TO THE ORIGINAL BUILDING MADE NECESSARY BY DIFFERENTIAL SETTLEMENT ASSOCIATED WITH PILE DAMAGE
PARTIAL ENVELOPE REPLACEMENT
 o	REMOVAL AND REPLACEMENT OF ARCHITECTURAL MOULDED CONCRETE TRIMS, METAL PANELS, AND SOME MASONRY AND GLASS BLOCK COMPONENTS DUE TO WATER INFILTRATION AND LACK OF EXPANSION CONTROL BETWEEN THE ORIGINAL STRUCTURE AND PRIOR ADDITIONS
ENCLOSURE OF THE COURTYARD COVERED PORCH
 o	REDUCE THE SIZE OF THE COURTYARD. THE COVERED PORCH WAS ORIGINALLY AN INTERIOR SPACE, THE SLAB INSIDE AND OUT IS AT THE SAME LEVEL WHICH HAS CAUSE SIGNIFICANT DAMAGES TO INTERIOR FINISHES DUE TO SPLASH BACK AND FLOODING
MISCELLANEOUS EXTERIOR ENVELOPE REPAIRS
 o	CLOSURE OF MISCELLANEOUS PENETRATIONS 
 o	REPAIR OF BROKEN MASONRY UNITS AND MORTAR
     	  o MASONRY SEALING
     	  o WINDOWSILL REPAIRS
INTERIOR FINISH AND FIXTURE REPAIRS/REPALACEMENT
 o	REPLACEMENT OF WALL BOARD AFFECTED BY MOISTURE INFILTRATION
     	o REPLACEMENT OF AGEING PLUMBING FIXTURES IN SITU
MECHANICAL EQUIPMENT REPLACEMENT
     o	REPLACEMENT OF FAILED OUTSIDE AIR UNIT
     o EXHAUST SYSTEM REPAIRS
   o MISCELLANEOUS REPAIRS TO VRF

name: 533 Royal - Updated Mechanical Drawings.pdf
description:
SUBSTANTIAL HISTORIC RENOVATION OF EXISTING FULLY SPRINKLERED 3-STORY BRICK MASONRY AND WOOD MUSEUM (CULTURAL FACILITY). NO CHANGE OF USE IS PROPOSED. THE CAMPUS IS COMPRISED OF SEVERAL BUILDINGS THAT HAVE BEEN ACQUIRED OVER TIME THAT ARE INTERCONNECTED. 2 AREAS OF NEW CONSTRUCTION WILL SLIGHTLY EXPAND INTO EXISTING NON-PERMEABLE COURTYARDS AND INCREASE THE OVERALL GROSS SQUARE FOOTAGE. STORMWATER WILL BE RETAINED IN UNDERGROUND TANKS WITHIN 2 COURTYARDS. THE SCOPE OF WORK INCLUDES SEVERAL NEW ELEVATORS AND REGRADING OF THE SITE TO MAKE THE CAMPUS ADA ACCESSIBLE. STRUCTURAL STABILIZATION WILL BE PERFORMED THROUGHOUT ALL BUILDINGS. THE CAMPUS WILL RECEIVE NEW MECHANICAL, ELECTRICAL, SPRINKLER, FIRE ALARM, STORMWATER, AND PLUMBING 
SYSTEMS. THE PROPERTIES WERE RESUBDIVIDED INTO ONE LOT OF RECORD AND CONSIDERED ONE BUILDING BY CODE.
ALL WORK TO BE DONE AS PER PLANS.

name: 2025-01-27-OHL-A-CD-DRAWING-SET-07-PLUMB-MECH.pdf
description: Odyssey House Louisana Tonti St. Campus is a Residential Care Facility (rehabilitative recovery) occupying various buildings on the site at 1125 N. Tonti St.  This project is a renovation of Building A, which is a 3-story-plus-attic historic building built in 1895.  Project scope is to renovate the building to accommodate common areas and administrative use on Level 1, sleeping units on Level 2 and 3, and common space and mechanical on Level 4 (attic).  Scope also includes site work including the execution of the remaining stormwater management plan work as submitted with Building E (permit ref code GHY0E6, no. 22-30761-RNVS).

name: 25001-Fight Back Center-Site Plan.pdf
description: Applying for zoning review and conditional use verification - Construction of a cultural facility constructed of modular pre-fabricated shipping container structures connected with a deck.  NOTE: CULTURAL FACILITY WAS NOT AN OPTION UNDER "USE" FOR THE APPLICATION, BUT IT IS A USE PER THE CZO.  Disregard the use "community center" that was selected. The programming for the facility will be for community meetings, live events, food and beverage sales, youth programming, and gathering.  The existing driveway will be re-used.  Parking will provided in the rear and the 10' buffer yard shall be provided as required per the CZO.

name: State Fire Marshal Equivalency Ferry Terminal Second Floor Stair.pdf
description:

A second floor, elevator and stair will be added to the existing ferry terminal building. Surrounding site, including the public right-of-way, will be renovated to create a new plaza area with new stairs and accessible sloped walks to the terminal building. All work to be done as per plans.

name: 2118-01_Temporary Classrooms Building_Good Shepherd School_Construction Documents_20250114_Volume 1.pdf
description: The move of the modular building was done under Permit #21-25670-TOVR and the permit was issued (see attached). In 2022 to an application for a renovation permit (Ref Code:P0RPHN) was filed for the building but the property owner (not GSS) didn’t have the funds to complete the work. Renovation permit (ref code: 1Y5NNR) was submitted in January but was denied because Jay considers the work to be new construction because the building was moved from one location to another. We are filing for a new construction permit to renovate the building and ensure building code life safety compliance for future use at its current location.

name: Newcomb Hall_Plumbing Dwgs-_NLH.pdf
description:
Partial interior renovation of historic (circa 1918) masonry building at Tulane University containing offices, classrooms and related support spaces, as described on the contract documents.  Project scope includes full renovation of 1st & 4th floors in their entirety, including replacement of all mechanical, electrical, plumbing and fire protection systems on those floors.  Project also includes limited (minor) interior alterations to Basement, 2nd & 3rd floors, a new elevator serving all five floors, upgrades to 2-hour rated stair enclosures at three existing stairwells, and two new light monitors at roof to provide daylight to 4th floor spaces.  Project includes limited site work associated with the installation of underground utilities.  The project does not include any exterior facade repairs or improvements. All work to be done as per plans. 

*Follow up with SWB for Approval

name: 520 Frenchmen St.pdf
description:
Construct a 3 story 11989 s.f. mixed used building as per plans, BZA#255-12 & 12-27386-HDLC 1st and 2nd floor: 7666 sq ft restaurant; 3rd floor: 3 apartments. (Signs require a seperate permit)

This TCO expires on 1/31/2015

During the term of this Temporary Occupancy, the following actions must be taken. Failure to complete these requirements will result in the rescission of occupancy following this 30-day period and no further occupancy will be granted until the structure is fully compliant:
1)	The building shall operate as a standard restaurant, in compliance with all standards of the Building Code, Comprehensive Zoning Ordinance, and the City Code.
2)	All building code deficiencies will be remedied, either through the Board of Building Standards and Appeals or through modifications to the structure.
3)	All deviations from HDLC approved plans will be remedied.
4)	Occupancy of the second floor shall not exceed 49 persons.
5)	No occupancy is granted for the third floor of the structure.
6)	Use of the bar on the second floor is prohibited under any and all circumstances
7)	Application will be filed for renewal of the variances granted under BZA Docket 255-12. Previously granted variances have expired.
8)	Application will be filed for the retention of the second floor holding bar which exceeds the square footage allowable pursuant to Section 11.51 of the CZO (alternatively this bar may be physically removed from the structure).

Construct a 3 story 11989 s.f. mixed used building as per plans, BZA#255-12 & 12-27386-HDLC 1st and 2nd floor: 7666 sq ft restaurant; 3rd floor: 3 apartments. (Signs require a seperate permit)

This TCO expires on 1/31/2015

During the term of this Temporary Occupancy, the following actions must be taken. Failure to complete these requirements will result in the rescission of occupancy following this 30-day period and no further occupancy will be granted until the structure is fully compliant:
1)	The building shall operate as a standard restaurant, in compliance with all standards of the Building Code, Comprehensive Zoning Ordinance, and the City Code.
2)	All building code deficiencies will be remedied, either through the Board of Building Standards and Appeals or through modifications to the structure.
3)	All deviations from HDLC approved plans will be remedied.
4)	Occupancy of the second floor shall not exceed 49 persons.
5)	No occupancy is granted for the third floor of the structure.
6)	Use of the bar on the second floor is prohibited under any and all circumstances
7)	Application will be filed for renewal of the variances granted under BZA Docket 255-12. Previously granted variances have expired.
8)	Application will be filed for the retention of the second floor holding bar which exceeds the square footage allowable pursuant to Section 11.51 of the CZO (alternatively this bar may be physically removed from the structure).

name: 14-1315-0070_Odyssey House Tonti St. Campus_Project Manual_Vol02.pdf
description: PROJECT DESCRIPTION:  BUILDING 'B' OF THE ODYSSEY HOUSE LOUISIANA CAMPUS LOCATED AT 1125 N. TONTI STREET, NEW ORLEANS IS APPROXIMATELY 6,723 SF WITHIN A TWO-STORY WOOD STRUCTURE.   THE ORIGINAL STRUCTURE DATES TO THE MID-1860'S, AND INCLUDED RESIDENTIAL SLEEPING QUARTERS AND A CHAPEL.   IT'S CURRENT USE INCLUDES A DEFUNCT KITCHEN, MEETING ROOMS AND OFFICES.   THE SCOPE OF WORK FOR THE PROJECT IS TO REDEVELOP THE FIRST FLOOR WITH A NEW KITCHEN (INCLUDING AN EXPANSION OF THE KITCHEN), FIRST FLOOR OFFICES, SECOND FLOOR SLEEPING ROOMS (WITH A MAXIMUM OF 4 BEDS PER ROOM.

description:
Remove temporary shoring that was put in place to stabilize the perimeter masonry walls under permit 18-34871-RNVS.  New work under this permit will include the new internal structure consisting of new foundation, upper floor framing and roof framing to replace the floor framing that collapsed. Work also includes underground electrical and plumbing service that would need to be in place prior to the new foundation.  A new elevator pit and two new fire exit stairs are included in the work.  This is a no occupancy permit and no mechanical work.  Interior build-out will be permitted separately. 

Benchmark certificate to be provided with occupancy permit.

description: VCC Application for building located at 533-537 Dumaine Street: to remove deteriorated balcony floorboards [3rd floor ONLY] and install new Aeratis Traditions T&G floorboards as per VCC Standard Detail Sheet No. 12 [to be obtained from VCC in advance of work]. name: 2019-07-31 - 106 Wren Street - SWMP Packet.pdf
description:
PHASE 2- New Construction Townhouse Development. 6 units in this phase for addresses 106-116 Wren Street. ( Phase I is RE 19-03458-NEWC). 

Stormwater plans for this project were initially reviewed and approved under Permit #19-03458-NEWC for Phase 1 and released subject to the condition that all improvements required were to be completed prior to C of O for any of the residential units. Consequently Permit 19-11600 was never approved or issued. The applicant has now requested that a portion of the units [Phase 1] be released prior to completion of the rest [Phase 2]. Due to the nature of the stormwater improvements, it is not feasible to complete everything before the associated buildings are done. Therefore, these 2 permits need to be separated in a manner which allows the Phase 1 units to be 100% compliant on their own. Improvements that depend upon the completion of the Phase 2 buildings and associated exterior features may then be legitimately deferred to the time of such completion.

name: 332 St. Joseph Greasy.pdf
description:
Proposal for new hotel within the footprint of an existing vacant storage building first used as a brewery. Site and building have long since been abandoned and new hotel will allow for adaptive re-use of the historic brewery structure. New construction will require full Demolition of 1 story warehouse building facing N. Diamond St and partial demotion of the existing 2 story warehouse building facing St Joseph St with retention of the front façade including cornice, windows, and exterior doors. 

The project consists of preserving the 2 story, 37’ tall, masonry building envelope along St. Joseph Street and building a new 5 story structure behind the historic building. This 5 story new structure is proposed from property line to property line on the Tchoupitoulas, S. Peters, and N. Diamond sides and proposes a 10’ setback from the St. Joseph Street façade on the 4th story and an additional 10’ setback at the 5th floor for a total setback of 20’. The new structure shall include a 5 level, 96 key hotel, a public lobby bar/restaurant, a rooftop bar/restaurant, and a rooftop pool that is open to the public.

name: 701 Tchoupitoulas - HDLC and RCC Stamped.pdf
description: The proposed work consists of the expansion of an existing gallery to the late 19th Century, two story, masonry and wood framed building. The expansion would add about 400 square feet to the existing 423 square feet gallery, which is on Tchoupitoulas Street. It would wrap around the corner onto Girod Street. The gallery would be a metal structure, with decorative metal posts, and handrails to match the existing gallery. New composite decking will be installed throughout the entire gallery. A set of French doors will be added at the location of an existing window to access the second floor of the gallery.

name: 2019.09.17 - VCC approved roof.pdf
description:
Remove existing roofing material,
Replace interior ridge beam, per attached materials stamped VCC approved 09/17/19.
Repair and/or replace underlayment as necessary,
Install new natural slate (grey/green), using copper nails,
Install new copper drip edge,
Install/repair half round copper gutters and flashing
Remove existing roof over front gallery and install new standing-seam copper roof. 
Install/reinstall ceramic or concrete V-style or terra cotta ridge tiles. Replace broken tiles as needed, to match existing.
Metal cap-flashing on the parapets or chimneys is not allowed.
Paint wooden trim and dormers to match existing, as needed.

Note:	Trash chutes are required for removal of debris from all roofs
	All work must conform to standard VCC policies & guidelines
	Permit does not allow for cap flashing on parapets, chimneys or surrounding walls
Torch-applied roofing is not permitted in the Vieux Carré. 

All repair/replacement work shall match existing in color, size, material, detail, profile and exposure.

NOTE: No HVAC platform or rooftop installation approved as part of this permit.

name: Building Permit.pdf
description:
Interior debris cleaning under permit #19-11495-DEMI.  In the course of this work, we discovered a great deal of ongoing water intrusion in some areas, which has led to a great deal of rot and significant structural damage.  This emergency permit is to replace these spots of significant structural damage with sound framing.  We will only put back framing that exactly matches the existing; there is no change in walls or openings or floor plan contemplated in this permit.  The areas of structural damage are highlighted on the attached floor plans.

No use or occupancy

name: SD117-20_Cert.pdf
description:
This development will consist of three, separate three-story hotel occupancies on three separate lots. Buildings B & C are 9,711 SF each and Building D is 8,201 SF. All buildings will have a concrete foundation, wood framing and an exterior finish of either stucco or brick veneer. There will be a shared parking lot behind the buildings. The property is currently an atypically shaped, thru-lot with fronts on Elysian Fields Ave, Chartres St. and Royal St. The lot will be subdivided into 5 lots to facilitate this development. Buildings A and E, indicated on the drawings, are future developments and are not in this scope of work. This project is being submitted at this time for ARC/HDLC review.

name: 930 Canal St - ARC review updated.pdf
description:
This project consists of the conversion of an existing unoccupied building into 4 new short term rental units with a commercial space below.

The work include's the removal of the existing partitions and stairs within. The facade plaster and windows will be rehabilitated. The entry storefront will be redesigned and a 20'-0" deep balcony will be added at the 2nd floor in keeping with the precedent at the adjacent property. A new commercial elevator and a new stair will be installed to serve egress requirements to the four rental units. Rooftop access will also be provided. The addition at the rear of the building is proposed to be extened at levels 2 & 3. 

The project will receive new mechanical equipment, electrical service and plumbing; the installation of a new fire alarm and sprinkler system. There is no rear yard at the ground floor.

name: CHATEAU MAGIC INFO.pdf
description:
This is an interior renovation of a two story, 836 SF Interior building; one of three buildings on this lot that used to be owned by Chef Paul Prudhomme and used as guest rooms adjacent to his residence.  It suffered water damage from an unrepaired roof and the renovation costs less than 50% of the previous value. Renovation includes framing, siding, flashing, new roof, gutters, electrical, HVAC, plumbing, drywall, painting, doors, windows, tile, flooring, cabinets, and stone.

No exterior work to main building approved under this permit.

name: 3625 St Charles - HDLC Approved documents.pdf
description:
Facade improvements: repaint all exterior wall and ceiling surfaces, including garage; repair and refinish existing balconies, including railing, soffits, fascia panels, etc., replace all windows; repair/replace framing, sheathing, cladding and flashing, as needed
Roofing improvements: remove existing spray applied roofing, foam, and substrate down to plywood decking; repair/replace any damaged decking and framing, and replace with a new SBS and tapered rigid foam system; replace mansard roofing material, underlayment and flashing
Entrance improvements: consolidate to a single entrance door at Amelia street; incorporate a new ADA compliant ramp and stair; replace/repair existing finishes in ground floor elevator lobby
Site: repair and refinish CMU privacy wall; new hardscaping and landscaping

name: 23_0214 - VCC Submittal - Roof.pdf
description: This application is being submitted for conceptual review by the Vieux Carré Comission Architectural Committee. The roof and all interior courtyards of the Upper Pontalba Building are in scope. The roof will be repaired to align with its historic condition. The courtyards will undergo a proposed renovation as indicated in the drawings, where 3 typical courtyard conditions are shown. The renovation will include reimplementation of the historic, original balcony in the courtyards. The remaining 11 courtyards will undergo similar renovations. Once conceptual approval of the typical courtyard designs is received, the remaining 11 courtyards will be submitted for VCC review at a later date when we submit the drawings to Safety and Permits.

name: 21_1109_PERMIT Artiststudio F.pdf
description: The renovation to a two story building at 1434 N. Rampart Street, New Orleans . The previous use of the building was believe to be a automotive upholstery shop, and then later becoming a medical center. The building has been vacant for 20 years and the proposed use  would be a Art studio and Gallery, with an office on the ground floor. The building is located in the Historic Marigny/Treme/Bywater Commercial Districts/HMC-2, which permits the use of an Art Studio, Gallery, and Office under section 10.2.A in the New Orleans Comprehensive Zoning Ordinance.

name: 1721-35 St. Andrew St_GeoTech Report.pdf
description: New Construction Planned Unit Development (P.U.D.) on a subdivided lot (originally 1721-35 St. Andrew St. and to be changed to 1725 St. Andrew St. Units A-P). Building 1 consists of seven (7) attached townhouse units (B-H) and is part of a Planned Unit Development project with 16 total units. Building 1 is one of four separate buildings on this P.U.D. project site. Attached Townhomes are 2 stories, with ground floor garage and driveway, and contain 2 bedrooms & 2 bathrooms each. Construction system consists of heavy timber structural frame, concrete grade beam foundation, CMU wall base, Structurally Insulated Panels (SIP), & Steel Stud walls, floors, and roof. Painted fiber cement board exterior wall finish and standing seam metal roof. Attached townhomes are sprinklered and each unit is separated by 1-hour rated demising walls.

name: Hurricane Damage_Proof of Occupancy.pdf
description:
This permit is for the renovation of an existing 41,703 square foot building owned by the American Red Cross. Project work is to include the conversion of the first-floor level office space into parking, partial replacement of existing first floor level storefront glazing with perforated metal screen, placement of two new exterior doors for vehicular access, replacement of existing chain link fencing with a new security fencing and gate, removal of existing ground level radio tower, and replacement of existing MEP systems throughout the facility. Painting of the existing exterior brick façade is under consideration.

Additional renovations include a complete interior renovation to the 2nd, 3rd, and 4th floors. At the time, the 3rd and 4th floors would be “shell space” for a future tenant, storage, and mechanical rooms. The 2nd floor renovation would primarily be used for administrative office space and training rooms by the American Red Cross.

name: 838 Canal_Exterior Renovations.pdf
description: Renovation of an existing 3-story masonry commercial building with 2224sf with a 188sf mezzanine. First floor build-out for a new restaurant. Work to include all new mechanical, electrical, and plumbing service, kitchen hood and exhaust, new finishes and modifications of the storefront. Provide a new enclosed stair from first floor to second floor. Join new stair enclosure with the existing stair flight to the third floor. Add new mechanical stair from the third floor to the roof for access to new rooftop mechanical equipment servicing new ground floor restaurant. Second and third floors are to remain vacant and will require separate permit(s) to build out. All work to be done as per plans.

name: AR-22-017599-101220220220.pdf
description:
The project is a new construction of a K-8 Educational Facility on University of New Orleans' campus. The submission is for the Student Dining/Commons building that is 1 story and approximately 10,500 SF and located behind the 3 story academic building (reviewed under permit # 22-28721). New parking on the west side of property for the new school facility and new parking on the north side will be exclusively for UNO. Existing parking on the east will be modified as necessary. The project includes all new electrical, mechanical, plumbing, fire protection, kitchen equipment, and security. All work to be done as per plans. 
Stormwater management is under 22-28721-NEWC

name: Benchmark.pdf
description:
Modular classroom buildings

Campus development plan review in accordance with Article 4, Section 4.12 and Article 20, Section 20.3.Z for a temporal modular classroom at Mildred Osborne Elementary School, an existing primary educational facility. Pursuant to Table 13-1, primary educational facilities are conditional uses within the site’s S-RS Single-Family Residential District. 

The temporal modular classroom would measure 24 ft in width, 64 ft in depth, and 1,536 sq ft in area. The temporal classroom will serve two kindergarten classrooms, that after two years will be located at another site. The modular classroom shall not affect existing parking or circulation as it will not be placed where it obstructs traffic. The existing school will remain. 


Eligible for 2year TCO once completed.

name: Building Permit.pdf
description: Homer Plessy Elementary School- This building is classified as EDUCATIONAL but there is no option under the Land Use pulldown menu for Educational.  The scope of work is as follows: replacement of all existing wood windows with wood clad metal windows of same profile and dimensions.  Replacement of damaged exterior wood doors with exterior grade metal doors and frames of same profile.  Main Entrance doors are to be repair and refinished.  Replacement of damaged wood rail system at rear two-story building.  Repairs to existing stucco facade due to water infiltration in various locations, repainting exterior of building to match existing.  Removal and replacement of existing roof systems.  Interior Scope of work: replacement of damaged lay-in ceilings, repairs/replacement of finish floors in certain areas (see submitted drawings), repairs and repainting of certain perimeter exterior walls due to water infiltration. Replacement of damaged window sills to match existing.

description:
This project consists of a two-story, 5,519 SF non-profit boathouse facility that's purpose is to engage with the public and become a learning center for people of all ages, demographics, and abilities. This facility will house offices and breakrooms for all of the staff, a multipurpose presentation room, and a first-floor workshop where sailboats will be repaired as needed.

name: Life Storage CDs 6-30-23.pdf
description:
The scope of work includes the demolition and reconstruction of fire damaged portion of the one-story pre-engineered metal storage building as indicated on the drawings, including: 
a. Remove and replacement of prefinished metal roof and wall panels.
b. Replacement of cold formed steel roof purlins, channel columns and miscellaneous framing channels.
c. Hollow metal frames and doors, including storage locker coiling overhead doors and corrugated metal swing doors.
d. Replacement of vinyl faced batt insulation at underside of roof system.
e. Replacement of electrical distribution panels, conduits, conductors, lighting, power and special systems.
f. Replacement of mechanical split systems and ductwork.

description: Earlier this year (2023) the entire exterior of the building was prepped to be waterproofed and painted. During this process, it was discovered that a portion of the exterior face of the building located on the left-hand/Upperline side of the building had deteriorated significantly. This led the association to hire the structural engineering services of Jamie Saxon from MMI to determine the scope of work needed for repairs. As these plans will be attached to the application, the simple plan and goal is to repair the face of the building in kind.

name: 2537 TULANE RESUB 23-005 SIGNED.pdf
description:
Construction of a single story shell building for a grocery store to be
built-out under separate contact. Work includes loading area, parking and interior drives and walks, perimeter
fencing, landscaping, site lighting and signage. Also included is the preparation of a building pad for a future out
building on the site. The grocery is to be steel framed with metal stud and CMU consruction, with stucco,
masonry and metal panel exterior, and membrane roofing. Shell building will include electrical service to building &
interior lighting, HVAC units, plumbing stubbed to building, fire sprinkler system and fire alarm.
(PERMIT TO CONSTRUCT BUILDING ONLY)
(INTERIOR BUILDOUT WILL BE DONE UNDER 24-14124-RNVN)
.

name: MARSALIS ELEVATOR CD 12224.pdf
description: The proposed scope of work consists of adding a new hydraulic elevator to an existing 3-story school building.  The elevator is to be erected on the exterior of the building, in the courtyard.  The new elevator shaft is to be supported by helical piles, and be constructucted of CMU, with a plaster finish.  The new roof will be an SBS membrane flat roof, with internal roof drains.  Three new openings will cut into the existing courtyard facade, to provide elevator access at each floor.  A one story equipment room will be built adjacent to the shaft, to hold the hydraulic pump, and controlers.

name: COTRACT EMERGENCY WORK.pdf
description: Emergency structural strengthening of the building brick walls, facades, interior walls, cracks mitigation, steel framing, new concrete footings to support the steel framing, fenestration change due to the failed lintels, and tie backs to distribute movement of the building. The roof repair to be done after with a new permit. The set of drawings attached indicate a survey of the building after hurricane Ida showing multiple and critical condition of the structural envelope of the building. The collapsed corners and lintel brick work was repaired and the steel supports were installed to prevent further deterioration. The contractors involved were Aubrey Howard Bricks and Masonry Products with the steel subcontractor. General Contractor will be submitted later. The Owner: Mr. Joseph J. Fertitta is the applicant to provide costs as per executed emergency construction work (527 At. Joseph St, New Orleans, La 70130).

name: Draft Minutes 8.21.24 .pdf
description: The Project consists of the construction of a new 2,140 SF facility serving the Softball Field and T-Ball Field at the Wesley Barrow Stadium property.  The building will contain a classroom, concession area, locker room and bathrooms.  The construction of the facility will be  metal framing installed on a raised concrete slab, with cement board siding and a metal standing seam roof.  Interior finishes will be gypsum board walls, gypsum board and acoustic tile lay-in ceiling, VCT flooring and rubber base. The building will have a new HVAC system and plumbing service as well as new electrical service and light fixtures. Minor site work will be done to install new sidewalks and to add parkingstripes / ADA parking to the existing asphalt parking lot. All Work will be per applicable codes and will be ADA compliant.  Refer to list of Alternates this sheet for possible additional scope to be added.

name: 2903 JEFFERSON_PHASE ONE. (RCC stamped).pdf
description: Phase One encompasses work required to make the facilities ready for a new congregation. This includes an overhaul of the mechanical systems serving the Educational Building and Gym/Fellowship Hall including both modified and entirely new systems. In addition to accounting for the power needs of new mechanical systems, the electrical scope includes swapping out existing light fixtures with new fixtures in the same location and providing new lighting and outlets to support services hosted in the Fellowship Hall. Plumbing upgrades include the relocation of an existing laundry room and the addition of two small restrooms in an area prior utilized as storage. Phase One also aims to address the roofing scope campus-wide to ensure watertight buildings before future improvements take place. Beyond system and envelope upgrades, interior finishes in these areas of the building will also be upgraded to avoid any future work requirements to the spaces in later phases of construction.

In addition to accounting for the power needs of new mechanical systems, the electrical scope includes swapping out existing light fixtures with new fixtures in the same location and providing new lighting and outlets to support services hosted in the Fellowship Hall. Plumbing upgrades include the relocation of an existing laundry room and the addition of two small restrooms in an area prior utilized as storage. Phase One also aims to address the roofing scope campus-wide to ensure watertight buildings before future improvements take place. Beyond system and envelope upgrades, interior finishes in these areas of the building will also be upgraded to avoid any future work requirements to the spaces in later phases of construction.

name: 3340 Orleans Ave New Orleans LA 70119 CBM Signed.pdf
description: The scope of work consists of structurally renovating an existing convenience store building, renovating the pump island fascias, and installing new signage. The existing building will be about 50% partially demolished by removing the existing roof framing and some interior non-load-bearing walls. An addition to increase the Convenience Store sales area will be constructed on the left side of the building and a new corrugated metal roof will be constructed. The existing CMU walls will remain in place. A knee wall will be built to raise the ceiling height from 9 feet to 14 feet. The old shed building on the left side will be removed to allow for the addition to the existing Convenience Store. New landscaping and sidewalk repair is proposed. Existing fuel pumps will remain. No new pumps will be installed. The owner is only painting the columns and repairing the canopy ceilings and replacing the fascia with new metal panels and new signage.

name: Building Permit.pdf
description:
THE INTENT OF THIS PROJECT IS TO PERFORM INTERIOR AND EXTERIOR REPAIRS RESULTING FROM A COMBINATION OF FOUNDATION AND ENVELOPE ISSUES. THE SCOPE OF WORK INCLUDES:
PILE REPAIR 
o	REPAIR OF COMPRIMISED PILES CONCENTRATED IN THE NORTHEAST CORNER OF THE BUILDING
STAIR TOWER REPAIR
 o	STRUCTURAL AND ENVELOPE REPAIRS TO THE NORTHEAST STAIR TOWER ADDITION TO THE ORIGINAL BUILDING MADE NECESSARY BY DIFFERENTIAL SETTLEMENT ASSOCIATED WITH PILE DAMAGE
PARTIAL ENVELOPE REPLACEMENT
 o	REMOVAL AND REPLACEMENT OF ARCHITECTURAL MOULDED CONCRETE TRIMS, METAL PANELS, AND SOME MASONRY AND GLASS BLOCK COMPONENTS DUE TO WATER INFILTRATION AND LACK OF EXPANSION CONTROL BETWEEN THE ORIGINAL STRUCTURE AND PRIOR ADDITIONS
ENCLOSURE OF THE COURTYARD COVERED PORCH
 o	REDUCE THE SIZE OF THE COURTYARD. THE COVERED PORCH WAS ORIGINALLY AN INTERIOR SPACE, THE SLAB INSIDE AND OUT IS AT THE SAME LEVEL WHICH HAS CAUSE SIGNIFICANT DAMAGES TO INTERIOR FINISHES DUE TO SPLASH BACK AND FLOODING
MISCELLANEOUS EXTERIOR ENVELOPE REPAIRS
 o	CLOSURE OF MISCELLANEOUS PENETRATIONS 
 o	REPAIR OF BROKEN MASONRY UNITS AND MORTAR
     	  o MASONRY SEALING
     	  o WINDOWSILL REPAIRS
INTERIOR FINISH AND FIXTURE REPAIRS/REPALACEMENT
 o	REPLACEMENT OF WALL BOARD AFFECTED BY MOISTURE INFILTRATION
     	o REPLACEMENT OF AGEING PLUMBING FIXTURES IN SITU
MECHANICAL EQUIPMENT REPLACEMENT
     o	REPLACEMENT OF FAILED OUTSIDE AIR UNIT
     o EXHAUST SYSTEM REPAIRS
   o MISCELLANEOUS REPAIRS TO VRF

FINISHES, NEW KITCHEN AND KITCHEN EQUIPMENT, NEW HVAC, AND ASSOCIATED 
MECHANICAL, PLUMBING, AND ELECTRICAL WORK IN BASEMENT AND FIRST FLOOR.

name: AR-24-020503- 234 Loyola SFM Released.pdf
description:
The proposed project involved the renovation of the 2nd and 3rd floors of two connected buildings collectively known as "The Pythian." This scope of work includes the following:
•	Interior Demolition: complete interior demolition of all elements on the 2nd and 3rd floors, including the removal of walls, ceilings, fixtures, and mechanical, electrical, and plumbing (MEP) systems. Demolition of existing elevator and stairs at mezzanine. 
•	Structural Modification: Deleting and adding new structural steel to receive new ADA ramp where elevator and stair are currently located. 
•	Conversion to Residential Units: Conversion of the 2nd and 3rd floors into residential units, with services and chases aligned vertically with the residential units on the upper floors. New Gym and Social Lounge Spaces.
•	Lightwell Extension: Extension of the existing lightwell to the 2nd and 3rd floors to provide natural light to the rear residential units. Partial Demo of existing concrete slab and roof system for the installation of a new roof and pedestal paver system at the lightwell extension. Steel beams modifications to accept new roof.
•	Exclusions: The ground floor market, lobby, bike room, floors 4-9, and rooftop are excluded from the renovation scope.

description:
SUBSTANTIAL HISTORIC RENOVATION OF EXISTING FULLY SPRINKLERED 3-STORY BRICK MASONRY AND WOOD MUSEUM (CULTURAL FACILITY). NO CHANGE OF USE IS PROPOSED. THE CAMPUS IS COMPRISED OF SEVERAL BUILDINGS THAT HAVE BEEN ACQUIRED OVER TIME THAT ARE INTERCONNECTED. 2 AREAS OF NEW CONSTRUCTION WILL SLIGHTLY EXPAND INTO EXISTING NON-PERMEABLE COURTYARDS AND INCREASE THE OVERALL GROSS SQUARE FOOTAGE. STORMWATER WILL BE RETAINED IN UNDERGROUND TANKS WITHIN 2 COURTYARDS. THE SCOPE OF WORK INCLUDES SEVERAL NEW ELEVATORS AND REGRADING OF THE SITE TO MAKE THE CAMPUS ADA ACCESSIBLE. SYSTEMS. THE PROPERTIES WERE RESUBDIVIDED INTO ONE LOT OF RECORD AND CONSIDERED ONE BUILDING BY CODE.
ALL WORK TO BE DONE AS PER PLANS.

Tonti St.  This project is a renovation of Building A, which is a 3-story-plus-attic historic building built in 1895.  Project scope is to renovate the building to accommodate common areas and administrative use on Level 1, sleeping units on Level 2 and 3, and common space and mechanical on Level 4 (attic). name: 2025.04.09_202423_Joe Brown Park_CD-100-compressed.pdf
description:
The proposed project is a new carousel enclosure in Joe W. Brown Park. The enclosure is to include a full scale, operational carousel, a concession and tickets stand (for selling prepackaged goods - no food prep), and gathering areas around the interior and exterior of the carousel enclosure. The carousel includes a roof and walls to protect from the elements, but is not an air tight enclosure. New electrical and plumbing service are to be run to the carousel enclosure.

PLEASE NOTE THE PERMIT ADDRESS HAD TO BE CHANGED DURING THE COURSE OF APPROVAL. THE CURRENT ADDRESS IS 50 JOE BROWN DR

The enclosure is to include a full scale, operational carousel, a concession and tickets stand (for selling prepackaged goods - no food prep), and gathering areas around the interior and exterior of the carousel enclosure. The carousel includes a roof and walls to protect from the elements, but is not an air tight enclosure. New electrical and plumbing service are to be run to the carousel enclosure.

PLEASE NOTE THE PERMIT ADDRESS HAD TO BE CHANGED DURING THE COURSE OF APPROVAL. THE CURRENT ADDRESS IS 50 JOE BROWN DR

The existing building was originally a parochial school, renovated in 2018 to a co-working office space. The 2018 renovation achieved Historic Tax Credits. Reconfigure circulation with the addition of an elevator to provide an accessible route to the second floor. The business occupancy of the building will remain same in the upcoming renovation. 

*MUST RESOLVE BACKFLOW PREVENTION PRIOR TO ANY INSPECTIONS*

Renovation to reconfigure the existing program spaces to house administrative offices, classroom and training spaces, fine arts studio classrooms, a performance arts rehearsal and performance spaces, and associated storage, bathroom, prep area, and support spaces. Reconfigure circulation with the addition of an elevator to provide an accessible route to the second floor. The business occupancy of the building will remain same in the upcoming renovation.

name: _Newcomb Hall PHASE 1 - Specs Vol 1 (BID DOCS).pdf
description:
Partial interior renovation of historic (circa 1918) masonry building at Tulane University containing offices, classrooms and related support spaces, as described on the contract documents.  Project scope includes full renovation of 1st & 4th floors in their entirety, including replacement of all mechanical, electrical, plumbing and fire protection systems on those floors.  Project also includes limited (minor) interior alterations to Basement, 2nd & 3rd floors, a new elevator serving all five floors, upgrades to 2-hour rated stair enclosures at three existing stairwells, and two new light monitors at roof to provide daylight to 4th floor spaces.  Project includes limited site work associated with the installation of underground utilities.  The project does not include any exterior facade repairs or improvements. All work to be done as per plans. 

*Follow up with SWB for Approval

Reinstall Fascia on completion.

description:
This application is supplemental to Non-Structural Renovation Permit #24-30862-RNVN. We uncovered termite damage during our original scope of work which requires framing repairs. We also have some layout updates as per owner request. 1. Remove an existing bedroom and bathroom that were previously adjacent to the pool area. 2. Add an interior stairwell to connect the two floors, including a new landing at the top of the stairs and a new door leading to the existing balcony.

name: 3909Magazine_AddressCert.pdf

name: 2212 S Claiborne CBM.pdf
description:
Restaurant Scope of Work: Structural Renovation and Building Improvements

The proposed project involves a comprehensive structural renovation and reconstruction of the existing building. The scope of work includes:
	•	Roof Replacement: Complete removal of the existing roof and installation of a new roofing system to ensure structural integrity and weather resistance.
	•	Siding Replacement: Removal and replacement of all exterior siding with updated, durable materials to improve both appearance and insulation.
	•	Exterior and Interior Painting: Full painting of the building’s exterior and interior surfaces to enhance curb appeal and interior aesthetics.
	•	Plumbing System Overhaul: Upgrading the plumbing system, including replacement of outdated pipes, fixtures, and water lines to meet current building codes and efficiency standards.
	•	Electrical System Update: Replacement and rewiring of the building’s electrical system, including new panels, outlets, switches, and lighting to ensure safety and code compliance.
	•	Drywall Installation: Repair or replacement of damaged Sheetrock (drywall) throughout the interior for a clean, finished look.
	•	Debris Removal and Site Cleanup: Proper disposal of construction debris and a thorough cleanup of the worksite during and after the renovation process.
     .   New   HVAC AND GAS system

This renovation is intended to improve the safety, functionality, and appearance of the property while bringing the structure up to modern

name: Building Permit.pdf
description: EMERGENCY PERMIT APPLICATION TO SHORE, REMOVE AND REPLACE WALL THAT IS IN IMMINENT DANGER OF COLLAPSING. According to the attached Engineer’s report dated July 14, 2025, the wall on the Toulouse Street side of the building bearing municipal address 601 Bourbon Street “is well outside of sound engineering and construction tolerances and should be replaced immediately” to prevent a collapse of the wall. This permit request includes the shoring of the Toulouse Street and Bourbon Street (partial) sides of the building; erection of a construction wall around the shoring area; removal of approximately 35 feet of the Toulouse Street wall to the parapet; recapping foundation rebar and concrete; rebuilding the wall three wythe thick; and related cosmetic restoration all in accordance with the attached stamped Engineer’s Masonry Wall Shoring and Repair Plans.

name: 601Bourbon - RCC Stamped.pdf
description: EMERGENCY PERMIT APPLICATION TO SHORE, REMOVE AND REPLACE WALL THAT IS IN IMMINENT DANGER OF COLLAPSING. According to the attached Engineer’s report dated July 14, 2025, the wall on the Toulouse Street side of the building bearing municipal address 601 Bourbon Street “is well outside of sound engineering and construction tolerances and should be replaced immediately” to prevent a collapse of the wall. This permit request includes the shoring of the Toulouse Street and Bourbon Street (partial) sides of the building; erection of a construction wall around the shoring area; removal of approximately 35 feet of the Toulouse Street wall to the parapet; recapping foundation rebar and concrete; rebuilding the wall three wythe thick; and related cosmetic restoration all in accordance with the attached stamped Engineer’s Masonry Wall Shoring and Repair Plans.

name: 3301 Magazine-L100.pdf
description:
RENOVATION AND EXPANSION OF AN EXISTING 1-STORY, 2,426 SF COMMERCIAL BUILDING TO INCLUDE A NEW SECOND STORY AND A TWO-STORY REAR ADDITION FOR A TOTAL OF 7045 SF. THE RECONFIGURED BUILDING WILL FEATURE A GROUND-FLOOR COMMERCIAL TENANT SPACE (TO BE BUILT OUT UNDER SEPARATE PERMITS) AND BUSINESS OCCUPANCY ON THE SECOND FLOOR (ALSO REQUIRING SEPARATE PERMITS TO BUILD OUT). SCOPE OF WORK INCLUDES SELECTIVE DEMOLITION, INSTALLATION OF NEW PILINGS AND FOUNDATIONS, CONVENTIONAL STEEL FRAMING, INTERIOR PARTITIONS, AND NEW PLUMBING, MECHANICAL, AND ELECTRICAL SYSTEMS. ALL WORK TO BE DONE AS PER PLANS
                                                                                                                                                                   BBSA NO. 23-25 APPROVAL GRANTED FOR EXISTING FIRST FLOOR ELEVATION TO REMAIN AT +5.38’ N.A.V.D.

HDLC – Ref Code: Y1Y0AY Partial demolition was approved in January 2023. The redesigned project has since been reviewed by Dennis Murphy, who confirmed it remains in compliance. The HDLC demolition permit is still active through January 2026.                                                                                                CPC – Design Advisory Committee – Ref Code: GKGR1V The revised project went before the DAC in May 2025 and received conditional approval, with the remaining items to be addressed at the staff level

name: 251010-SBC-PERMIT-2 of 2.pdf
description: Renovation and addition to the existing Sixth Baptist Church located at 928 Felicity Street, New Orleans, LA. Work includes interior renovation of the existing 2,880 square foot single-story sanctuary, demolition of an existing 1,500 square foot rear structure, and construction of a new two-story rear addition totaling approximately 3,080 square feet (2,200 square feet on the first floor and 880 square feet on the second floor) to accommodate expanded sanctuary space, restrooms, and storage. The project also includes site improvements with new parking, fencing, landscaping, and sidewalks, as well as upgraded mechanical, electrical, and plumbing systems throughout the facility

name: 907 Decatur_VCC submittal_102225.pdf
description:
To repair and renovate building as per plans. Work includes:
Installation of three new 8” round tie backs on the Dumaine elevation. Tie backs to be painted to match the building’s stucco, sounding and removal of any damaged or missing stucco to be repaired with new stucco, repair/replacement of wood windows to match existing, installation of new steel lintel at ground floor opening and repair of surrounding stucco to match adjacent openings, repair shutters at the second and third floor of the Decatur St. elevation to match existing, removal of the rooftop mechanical unit currently servicing the neighboring building at 911 Decatur St., repair existing slate roof to match existing, at rear flat roof, coat existing TPO roofing with 100% silicone roof coating for TPO, remove light fixtures, install two new can lights to the underside of the gallery, and place all wiring in conduit.

name: IRIS MOTEL - PERMIT DOCS - 12.22.25 COMBINED-compressed.pdf
description:
The Iris Motel project proposes exterior improvements and a small accessory building addition to enhance the guest experience and expand the property’s hospitality offerings. The work focuses on transforming a portion of the existing pool deck and parking lot into an inviting outdoor gathering space featuring a new bar, kitchen, and landscaped patio.

The project includes the renovation of approximately 1,000 square feet of existing site area, including extension of the existing outdoor pool deck and a portion of the existing parking lot. The design scope provides for an approximately 500-square-foot accessory building containing a bar and kitchen, a partially covered outdoor patio, and new restrooms. Site upgrades include selective demolition, PERMEABLE PAVING, landscape planting, AND NEW SITE lighting. Seven existing parking spaces will be retained to satisfy minimum parking requirements per the New Orleans Comprehensive Zoning Ordinance (CZO).

*FINAL INSPECTION* New Accessory Building needs both a FEMA EC and a FEMA Dry Floodproofing Certificate certified by registered professional with floodproofing up to 0.8 NAVD

name: photo 14 - breezeway damage (incl. windows).pdf
description: 16 unit apartment complex fire incident on 01.31.26. Two apartments & the entire roof sustained structural damage. The roof has already been removed, all apartments have been vacated & tenants relocated. The entire building has been blue tarped & secured after the clean up as of 02.04.2026. HVAC contractor removed 12 of the 16 ac condensors in order to deter theft the day after the fire and we will be reusing. Met with an engineer on 02.05.26 and is currently in the process of making plans/drawings to reconstruct the roof and re-build the two damaged apartments. The other apartments that sustained minor damage will also need renovations.

name: 2026-0309_Newman-ph2-Jefferson-Lemle_CD-Permit-Vol-1.pdf
description:
The Isidore Newman School Jefferson Building Restoration/Renovation centers on the partial renovation of the Jefferson Building with connections to the Saratoga Building. Improvements include partial demolition of interior partitions to restore historic architectural features and provide new office and student spaces. The project includes mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and fire protection systems in selected areas of the Jefferson and Saratoga buildings. Exterior renovations consist of façade refinishing work of plaster, a limited scope of window and door replacement, roof repair with dormer additions, and remodeling an outdoor courtyard.

BACKFLOW PREVENTION REVIEW REQUIRED PRIOR TO INSPECTIONS BEING ACCEPTED
